text: During laparoscopy, the surgeon observes a frozen pelvis with adhesions involving bowel loops, obscuring the visualization of the uterus, bilateral tubes, and ovaries.
The surgeon begins adhesiolysis, applying traction and counter traction techniques to carefully separate adhesions within the pelvic cavity.
The surgeon begins neutralization from a normal area, applying traction to reveal a white line between two structures, indicating a clear plane of dissection.
During the adhesiolysis procedure for endometriosis, the surgeon uses cold scissors to carefully dissect and spare the peritoneum, minimizing lateral thermal damage.
The surgeon uses cold scissors to dissect in the correct planes, carefully avoiding adhesions and preventing blood loss or injury to adjacent structures.

The surgeon visualizes the uterus and prepares to inject vasopressin into the myelodendron, using a dilution of twenty units in one hundred ml or forty units in two hundred ml, to aid in adhesiolysis and reduce blood loss.
